steps.title: Identify the paradox
steps.content: The key signal is the contrast: minimal guidance to the seeds yet high recruitment success—many recruits had the desired characteristics, far exceeding chance probability. Why?

steps.title: Match the explanation
steps.content: B explains it: the seeds' social networks include a high proportion of people who share characteristics with the seeds themselves. Since the seeds have the desired characteristics, their networks naturally contain others with those traits, explaining the high success rate without detailed instructions. The other options either contradict this (diverse networks, unaware participants) or introduce irrelevant factors (network size differences). That matches choice B — B is correct.
